problem.title: Linked List Cycle
problem.description: Given head, the head of a linked list, determine if the linked list has a cycle in it.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Floyd's Cycle Finding
solutions.description: Use Floyd's tortoise-and-hare algorithm with two pointers: a slow pointer advancing one step and a fast pointer advancing two steps per iteration. If a cycle exists the fast pointer will eventually lap the slow pointer and they will meet; if there is no cycle the fast pointer will reach the end of the list. This detects cycles in O(n) time with O(1) extra space.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import Optional

# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
#         self.val = val; self.next = next

class Solution:
    def hasCycle(self, head: Optional['ListNode']) -> bool:
        slow = head
        fast = head

        while fast is not None and fast.next is not None:
            slow = slow.next
            fast = fast.next.next
            if slow is fast:
                return True

        return False

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Floyd's Cycle Finding
solutions.description: Use Floyd's tortoise-and-hare algorithm with two pointers: a slow pointer advancing one step and a fast pointer advancing two steps per iteration. If a cycle exists the fast pointer will eventually lap the slow pointer and they will meet; if there is no cycle the fast pointer will reach the end of the list. This detects cycles in O(n) time with O(1) extra space.
